steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The passage sets up a contrast: Baroja's works are not especially controversial today, but we should consider what readers of his time may have perceived.

The blank needs a word describing how his contemporaries saw his work—specifically, something that would have made it stand out or matter to them then, even if we don't react that way now.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Provocative means likely to provoke strong reactions or disagreement—the exact quality that would explain why his contemporaries found aspects of his work striking and noteworthy.

Irrefutable addresses certainty rather than reader response, irrelevant means unconnected to the passage's point entirely, and pretentious suggests only mild stylistic criticism, not the kind of challenge that sticks with literary history.

That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
